prompt: Create a dynamic pop-art 3D illustration of exactly 36 Japanese maneki-neko lucky cat figurines exploding outward through a white space, as if floating in zero gravity. The main subject is one giant glossy ceramic lucky cat in the center foreground, smiling, with a raised left paw, white body covered in fine black contour-line engraving, red inner ears, yellow-black eyes, a pink nose, black whisker marks, a red collar with gold studs and a gold bell, gold oval chest medallion with bold black Japanese-style characters, and orange-black circular coin-like spots on the body. Surround it with 35 additional maneki-neko figurines at different scales and rotations: 6 large cats near the edges, 12 medium cats scattered through the middle depth, and 17 tiny cats receding into the background. All cats share the same white ceramic, red collar, gold bell, gold medallion, yellow eyes, red ears, and graphic contour-line texture; vary their orientation so some are upside down, sideways, cropped by the frame, or flying toward the viewer. Use a tall vertical poster canvas, 2:3 vertical, with an energetic wide-angle perspective and strong depth, the central cat occupying most of the middle. The background is pure white filled with explosive graphic design elements: hot magenta diagonal bars, neon yellow slashes, black speed lines, cyan/magenta/yellow halftone dots, radial arcs, comic-book motion streaks, and layered glitch-like offset outlines around objects. Use bright CMYK print colors, high contrast, crisp detail, glossy reflections, playful maximalism, and a futuristic Japanese pop-art advertising style. Add subtle chromatic aberration and rainbow edge fringing on the cats and linework. Include small printed text in the bottom-right corner reading May 14, 2026 above a loose black cursive artist signature Oyagi. Keep the composition clean enough that every cat remains recognizable, with no humans, no scenery, no extra text, and no watermark.
